task_completion.description: The agent opens and reviews the provided datasheet excerpt and the BME280 C driver source code.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or IDE showing the C source files in /home/ga/workspace/bme280_driver/ and a PDF viewer or text editor showing the datasheet excerpt.

task_completion.description: The agent fixes the control register initialization sequence bug in the C code.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor showing modifications to the register writing sequence, specifically targeting control registers or the device ID check (BME280 ID is 0x60).

task_completion.description: The agent fixes the bitwise precedence and humidity endianness (MSB/LSB swap) bugs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor showing added parentheses for bitwise operations and/or corrected bit-shifting logic for the humidity data parsing.

task_completion.description: The agent successfully compiles the modified C driver without errors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a compilation command (e.g., 'make' or 'gcc') executing and returning to the prompt without displaying compilation errors.

task_completion.description: The agent runs 'make test' which produces correct JSON output with realistic physical values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the execution of 'make test' followed by standard JSON output containing realistic environmental data (e.g., temperature, pressure, humidity), rather than impossible/garbage values.

integrity.description: The agent actually modified the C source files rather than creating a mock executable.
integrity.visual_evidence: Visible use of a text editor (like vim, nano, or VS Code) or standard file-editing commands modifying the actual .c files in the workspace.

integrity.description: The agent compiled the code using the standard build process.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal history showing the actual invocation of the compiler or build system (e.g., 'make') rather than just renaming a pre-compiled binary.

integrity.description: The JSON output is generated by the compiled C program and not a hardcoded script.
integrity.visual_evidence: The output in the terminal directly follows the 'make test' command, with no evidence of the agent using 'echo' or a separate Python script to print a fake JSON string.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The BME280 device ID is 0x60 (unlike the BMP280 which is 0x58). The specific bugs in the C implementation that need fixing are: 1) an incorrect initialization sequence for the control registers, 2) bitwise operation precedence errors, and 3) an endianness issue where the MSB and LSB are swapped for the humidity calculation. A successful fix will result in 'make test' outputting valid JSON with realistic physical values.
